title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a family day at a museum.
audio_script: Woman: Hello everyone, and thank you for coming to our museum family day. I'm going to explain what is happening so that you can plan your visit. The first event is a talk about old toys. It will be given by Dr Helen [14]Moore[/14], who has written several books about children's games in the past. The talk begins at half past ten in Room Two. After that, children can make paper animals in the education room. This activity is suitable for children aged [15]seven[/15] and above, because younger children may find the cutting difficult. At twelve o'clock, there will be music in the entrance hall. The performers are from a local [16]school[/16], and they have been practising for several weeks. If you want lunch, the museum café is open, but today it is serving only soup and sandwiches. Families may also eat their own food in the [17]courtyard[/17]. Please do not eat in the exhibition rooms. In the afternoon, there is a treasure hunt around the building. You can collect the question sheet from the [18]shop[/18]. Everyone who finishes it will get a small prize. Finally, the museum closes at [19]four thirty[/19] today because staff need to prepare for an evening event. There are volunteers around the museum wearing blue badges, and they can show you where to go. Some rooms may become crowded, so please be patient if you have to wait. We have tried to plan the day so families can move around easily without rushing. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.
